title: Telosma odoratissima
intro: Telosma odoratissima, also known as the fragrant Telosma or queen of the night, is a flowering plant species in the family Asclepiadaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Telosma odoratissima include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Telosma odoratissima is commonly prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin infections.
taxonomy: Telosma odoratissima is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is commonly known as the 'Kantutu' or 'Saffronvine'. This plant is often cultivated for its attractive yellow flowers and sweet fragrance. The genus Telosma is a small group of flowering plants, with Telosma odoratissima being one of the most well-known species. Telosma odoratissima is classified as a perennial climber, thriving in warm and humid environments.
uses: Telosma odoratissima, also known as the evening star or queen of the night, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
